name: Greek Chicken Salad
description: With Feta, Olives & Lemon Dressing
steps: Slice the chicken breast into strips and season with salt and pepper.
Heat one tablespoon of olive oil in a pan and cook the chicken until golden and cooked through.
Whisk the lemon juice with the remaining olive oil to make a simple dressing.
Arrange the baby spinach, cherry tomatoes, cucumber and red onion in bowls.
Add the warm chicken on top of the salad.
Scatter over the feta and black olives.
Drizzle with the lemon dressing and toss lightly.
Serve straight away while the chicken is still warm.
tip: Keep the chicken in large strips so it stays juicy and slices neatly through the salad.

name: BBQ Salmon with Spanish Rice
description: With Tenderstem Broccoli, Mange Tout & Lemon
steps: Cook the Spanish rice according to the packet instructions and keep it warm.
Toss the tenderstem broccoli and mange tout with olive oil, garlic and seasoning.
Roast or pan-fry the vegetables until just tender and lightly charred.
Brush the salmon fillets with a little olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Cook the salmon on a hot grill or griddle until the fish flakes easily.
Squeeze over fresh lemon juice and sprinkle with chopped dill.
Fluff the rice and fold through a little extra lemon zest if you like.
Serve the salmon over the rice with the vegetables alongside.
tip: A very hot pan gives the salmon a better BBQ-style finish without drying it out.

name: Chicken Pitta with Tzatziki
description: With Salad, Hummus, Tomatoes & Red Onion
steps: Season the chicken and cook it in a hot pan until browned and fully cooked.
Warm the wholemeal pittas so they are soft and ready to fill.
Slice the tomatoes and red onion and toss them with the mixed salad leaves.
Stir a little lemon juice into the tzatziki for extra freshness.
Split the pittas and spread each one with hummus.
Fill with salad, chicken, feta and red onion.
Spoon in the tzatziki and finish with a squeeze of lemon.
Serve immediately while the pittas are warm.
tip: Warm the pittas just before serving so they do not split when filled.

name: BBQ Chicken Souvlaki
description: With Tzatziki, Greek Salad & Warm Pitta
steps: Cut the chicken into strips and marinate with olive oil, garlic, oregano, lemon, salt and pepper.
Cook the chicken on a hot griddle or barbecue-style pan until nicely charred.
Prepare a Greek salad with cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, feta and olives.
Warm the pittas in a dry pan or oven.
Stir the tzatziki so it is smooth and ready to spoon over the chicken.
Pile the salad into each pitta and top with the chicken strips.
Add a generous spoonful of tzatziki and a little extra lemon if needed.
Serve with the remaining salad on the side.
tip: If time allows, marinate the chicken for 30 minutes before cooking for extra flavour.

name: Mediterranean Chicken Wrap
description: With Hummus, Feta & Mixed Salad
steps: Season and cook the chicken until golden and cooked through.
Warm the wraps so they are easier to fold.
Mix the salad leaves with cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion.
Spread each wrap with hummus.
Add the chicken on top of the hummus layer.
Pile in the salad and crumble over the feta.
Drizzle with a little lemon juice and olive oil.
Roll up tightly and serve.
tip: Keep the filling just off-centre so the wrap rolls more neatly.

name: Turkey & Bean Chilli
description: With Rice & Fresh Coriander
steps: Cook the basmati rice and keep it covered so it stays fluffy.
Heat the olive oil in a pan and soften the onion and red pepper.
Add the garlic and turkey mince, breaking the meat up as it browns.
Stir in smoked paprika, cumin and chilli flakes.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and simmer for a few minutes.
Add the red kidney beans and cook until the chilli thickens.
Season to taste and stir through most of the fresh coriander.
Serve the chilli over the rice with the remaining coriander on top.
tip: Let the chilli simmer uncovered at the end so the sauce reduces cleanly.

name: Tuna & Cannellini Bean Salad
description: With Cherry Tomatoes, Rocket & Lemon Dressing
steps: Season the tuna steaks and sear them quickly in a hot pan.
Drain and rinse the cannellini beans.
Whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, capers, salt and pepper.
Slice the cherry tomatoes, cucumber and red onion.
Toss the rocket with the beans and vegetables in a large bowl.
Slice the tuna and lay it over the salad.
Scatter over the chopped parsley and drizzle with the lemon dressing.
Serve while the tuna is still warm.
tip: Do not overcook the tuna; it should stay tender in the centre.

name: BBQ Mackerel with Spanish Rice
description: With Tenderstem Broccoli, Mange Tout & Lemon
steps: Cook the Spanish rice until fluffy and keep it warm.
Toss the broccoli and mange tout with olive oil, garlic, salt and pepper.
Cook the vegetables quickly so they stay bright and crisp.
Season the mackerel fillets and cook them skin-side down first.
Turn the fish to finish cooking through.
Squeeze over lemon juice and add the chopped dill.
Fluff the rice and check the seasoning of the vegetables.
Serve the mackerel with the rice and vegetables on the side.
tip: Mackerel benefits from a very hot pan so the skin crisps before the flesh overcooks.

name: Chicken Shakshuka
description: With Feta & Crusty Bread
steps: Cook the chicken in a large pan until lightly browned and almost cooked through.
Add olive oil, onion and red pepper and soften them for a few minutes.
Stir in the garlic, smoked paprika and cumin.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and simmer until the sauce thickens slightly.
Make four small wells in the sauce and crack in the eggs.
Cover the pan and cook until the egg whites are set but the yolks are still soft.
Scatter the feta over the top and warm the pittas separately.
Serve the shakshuka with the pittas for scooping.
tip: Keep the heat fairly low once the eggs go in so the yolks stay soft.

name: Pesto Basa Pasta
description: With Cherry Tomatoes & Parmesan
steps: Cook the penne in salted water until al dente.
Heat a little olive oil in a pan and soften the onion and garlic.
Add the basa fillets and cook gently until they start to flake.
Stir in the green pesto and cherry tomatoes.
Add the baby spinach and let it wilt into the sauce.
Drain the pasta and toss it through the fish and pesto mixture.
Finish with chopped basil and grated parmesan.
Serve immediately while the sauce is glossy and warm.
tip: Fold the fish into the pasta gently so the basa stays in large flakes.

name: Thai Fishcakes
description: With Sweet Chilli Dip & Asian Slaw
steps: Flake the pollock and mix it with the egg, breadcrumbs, panko, spring onions and coriander.
Season the mixture with salt, pepper and a squeeze of lime.
Shape into small fishcakes and chill briefly if the mixture feels soft.
Mix the Greek yoghurt, honey, chilli flakes and a little lime juice for the dip.
Combine the carrot, cucumber and red onion with sesame oil, soy sauce and lime for the slaw.
Shallow-fry the fishcakes until crisp and golden on both sides.
Serve the fishcakes with the sweet chilli dip and the slaw on the side.
Finish with extra coriander if you want a fresher flavour.
tip: Chilling the fishcakes for a few minutes helps them hold together in the pan.

name: Herb Crusted Cod
description: With Garlic Roasted Vegetables
steps: Preheat the oven and chop the courgette, aubergine and red pepper into even pieces.
Toss the vegetables with olive oil, garlic, salt and pepper.
Roast the vegetables until tender and lightly browned.
Mix the breadcrumbs with chopped parsley and thyme.
Brush the cod with a little olive oil and press the herb crumb onto the top.
Bake the cod until it flakes easily and the topping is golden.
Squeeze lemon over the fish and vegetables.
Serve the cod with the garlic roasted vegetables.
tip: Cut the vegetables to a similar size so they roast evenly.

name: Mediterranean Baked Salmon
description: With Roasted Vegetables & Herby Potatoes
steps: Cook the couscous and fluff it with a fork once the grains are tender.
Chop the courgette, aubergine, pepper, cherry tomatoes and red onion into roasting pieces.
Toss the vegetables with olive oil, oregano, salt and pepper.
Roast the vegetables until softened and lightly caramelised.
Season the salmon and place it on top of the tray for the final part of cooking.
Bake until the salmon is just cooked through.
Finish with lemon juice and chopped basil and parsley.
Serve the salmon with the vegetables and couscous.
tip: Add the salmon part-way through roasting so it stays tender and does not dry out.

name: Halloumi & Roasted Vegetable Couscous
description: With Lemon & Fresh Herbs
steps: Cook the couscous, then fluff it with a fork and keep it warm.
Chop the vegetables into bite-sized pieces and toss with olive oil, salt and pepper.
Roast the vegetables until soft and slightly browned.
Slice the halloumi and fry it until golden on both sides.
Add the roasted vegetables to the couscous and mix well.
Stir through lemon juice and chopped parsley and mint.
Top with the halloumi slices.
Serve warm with an extra squeeze of lemon.
tip: A hot pan gives halloumi the best golden crust.

name: Lemon Garlic Prawn Orzo
description: With Cherry Tomatoes & Fresh Parsley
steps: Cook the orzo in salted water until tender.
Heat olive oil in a pan and soften the garlic for a minute.
Add the cherry tomatoes and cook until they begin to burst.
Stir in the prawns and cook until they turn pink.
Add the baby spinach and let it wilt into the pan.
Drain the orzo and toss it through the prawn mixture.
Finish with lemon juice and chopped parsley.
Serve immediately while the orzo is still glossy.
tip: Keep the heat moderate once the prawns go in so they stay tender.

name: Chicken Shawarma Bowls
description: With Couscous, Salad & Garlic Yoghurt
steps: Mix the chicken with olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, ground coriander, garlic, salt and pepper.
Cook the chicken in a hot pan until browned and cooked through.
Prepare the couscous according to the packet instructions.
Chop the salad leaves, cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion.
Stir lemon juice into the Greek yoghurt to make a quick garlic yoghurt.
Fluff the couscous and divide it between bowls.
Add the salad and top with the chicken.
Spoon over the garlic yoghurt and serve.
tip: Toast the spices briefly with the chicken to deepen the shawarma flavour.

name: Healthy Chicken Gyros
description: With Homemade Tzatziki, Salad & Warm Pitta
steps: Season the chicken with olive oil, oregano, salt and pepper.
Cook the chicken in a hot pan until it is browned and cooked through.
Mix the salad leaves, cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion in a bowl.
Warm the pittas.
Stir a little lemon juice into the tzatziki for a brighter flavour.
Fill each pitta with the salad and chicken.
Add the feta and spoon over the tzatziki.
Serve immediately while the pittas are still warm.
tip: Slice the chicken thinly after cooking so the gyros are easier to assemble.

name: Turkey Meatballs in Tomato Sauce
description: With Wholemeal Pasta & Parmesan
steps: Mix the turkey mince with the egg, breadcrumbs, parmesan, salt and pepper.
Shape the mixture into meatballs.
Brown the meatballs in olive oil in a large pan.
Add the onion and garlic and soften them gently.
Pour in the passata and simmer until the sauce thickens.
Cook the wholemeal penne until al dente.
Toss the pasta through the sauce and meatballs and add the basil.
Serve with extra parmesan scattered on top.
tip: Wet your hands slightly when rolling the meatballs to stop the mixture sticking.

name: Baked Feta Pasta
description: With Roasted Cherry Tomatoes & Fresh Basil
steps: Heat the oven and place the cherry tomatoes, onion and garlic in a baking dish.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Nestle the block of feta into the centre of the dish.
Bake until the tomatoes are soft and the feta is melting.
Cook the penne while the tomatoes roast.
Mash the feta and tomatoes together to form a sauce.
Toss the pasta through the sauce and add the basil.
Serve immediately.
tip: Reserve a little pasta water so the feta sauce turns silky rather than dry.

name: Spinach & Chickpea Curry
description: With Basmati Rice & Fresh Coriander
steps: Cook the basmati rice and keep it covered while you make the curry.
Heat the olive oil and soften the onion in a large pan.
Add the garlic, turmeric, cumin, ground coriander and chilli flakes.
Stir in the chickpeas and chopped tomatoes.
Simmer until the sauce thickens slightly.
Add the baby spinach and let it wilt into the curry.
Check the seasoning and stir through most of the fresh coriander.
Serve the curry over the rice with the remaining coriander on top.
tip: If the curry seems too thick, loosen it with a splash of water before serving.

name: Mediterranean Chicken Traybake
description: A one-pan Mediterranean chicken dinner with peppers, tomatoes and herbs.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Slice the peppers and onion, then halve the cherry tomatoes.
Arrange the vegetables in a roasting tray with the garlic cloves.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with paprika, salt and pepper.
Place the chicken on top and scatter over the rosemary.
Roast for 30–35 minutes until the chicken reaches 75°C internally.
Rest the chicken for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve with the roasted vegetables and pan juices.
tip: Add feta for extra richness if serving guests.

name: Lemon Herb Chicken Skewers
description: Juicy chicken skewers marinated with herbs, garlic and lemon.
steps: Cut the chicken into bite-sized cubes.
Mix olive oil, garlic, lemon juice and chopped herbs.
Coat the chicken in the marinade.
Leave to marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Thread the chicken onto skewers.
Cook on a hot grill or BBQ for 12–15 minutes, turning regularly.
Check the chicken reaches 75°C internally.
Serve with salad or couscous.
tip: Soak wooden skewers for 30 minutes before grilling.

name: Greek Chicken Salad
description: A fresh Greek-inspired salad with grilled chicken and feta.
steps: Season the chicken with salt and pepper.
Grill the chicken until cooked through, then rest.
Prepare the salad vegetables.
Whisk together olive oil and lemon juice.
Slice the rested chicken.
Arrange the salad on serving plates.
Top with chicken, feta and olives.
Drizzle with dressing before serving.
tip: Warm chicken contrasts beautifully with the cold salad.

name: Turkey Koftas with Herby Couscous
description: Lean turkey koftas served with lemon and herb couscous and yoghurt.
steps: Mix the turkey mince with garlic, parsley, mint, cumin, coriander, salt and pepper.
Shape into eight small koftas.
Cook the couscous according to the packet instructions.
Fluff the couscous with chopped herbs and lemon zest.
Heat a lightly oiled grill pan or BBQ.
Cook the koftas for 15–18 minutes, turning regularly.
Mix the Greek yoghurt with a squeeze of lemon juice.
Serve the koftas over the couscous with the yoghurt.
tip: These freeze well before cooking.

name: Chicken Souvlaki
description: Greek-inspired marinated chicken skewers with fresh salad.
steps: Cube the chicken breast evenly.
Combine olive oil, lemon juice, garlic and herbs.
Marinate the chicken for at least 30 minutes.
Thread the chicken onto skewers.
Heat the BBQ or grill to a high heat.
Cook for 12–15 minutes until the chicken reaches 75°C.
Prepare a tomato and cucumber salad.
Serve immediately with the salad.
tip: Metal skewers give the best browning.

name: Pollock with Pesto Pasta
description: Pan-fried pollock served over basil pesto pasta with cherry tomatoes.
steps: Bring a pan of salted water to the boil.
Cook the pasta until al dente.
Season the pollock with salt and pepper.
Pan-fry the pollock until golden and cooked through.
Halve the cherry tomatoes and warm them gently.
Drain the pasta and stir through the pesto.
Add the tomatoes and grated Parmesan.
Top with the pollock and garnish with fresh basil.
tip: Cod or haddock can be substituted if pollock isn't available.

name: Herb Crusted Cod
description: Oven baked cod with a crisp herb crumb, served with green vegetables.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Mix breadcrumbs, Parmesan and chopped herbs.
Season the cod fillets.
Brush the fish with olive oil.
Press the breadcrumb mixture onto the fish.
Bake for 18–20 minutes until the fish flakes easily.
Steam the broccoli until tender.
Serve the cod alongside the broccoli.
tip: A squeeze of lemon before serving brightens the flavours.

name: Thai Fishcakes
description: Fresh homemade fishcakes with herbs and a light Thai flavour.
steps: Roughly chop the pollock.
Blend with garlic, coriander, fish sauce and chilli.
Mix through the egg and breadcrumbs.
Shape into six fishcakes.
Chill for 15 minutes.
Pan fry in a little sesame oil until golden.
Turn carefully and cook through.
Serve with lime wedges and salad.
tip: Wet hands make shaping the fishcakes much easier.

name: Mediterranean Baked Salmon
description: Salmon baked with tomatoes, olives and herbs.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Place the salmon in a baking dish.
Scatter over tomatoes, olives and garlic.
Drizzle with olive oil.
Season well.
Bake for 18–20 minutes.
Finish with chopped basil and lemon juice.
Serve immediately.
tip: Delicious served with steamed green beans or brown rice.

name: Grilled Mackerel with Herby Potatoes
description: Omega-3 rich mackerel served with crushed baby potatoes and fresh herbs.
steps: Cook the potatoes until tender.
Heat the grill or BBQ.
Season the mackerel fillets.
Grill the fish skin-side down first.
Drain and lightly crush the potatoes.
Mix the potatoes with herbs, olive oil and lemon.
Cook the fish until just flaky.
Serve immediately.
tip: Excellent cooked over charcoal.

name: Mediterranean Chickpea Stew
description: A hearty tomato and chickpea stew packed with Mediterranean flavours.
steps: Heat the olive oil in a saucepan.
Cook the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the paprika and oregano.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes.
Add the chickpeas.
Simmer gently for 20 minutes.
Finish with chopped parsley.
Serve with crusty bread or couscous.
tip: Even better the following day.

name: One-Pan Ratatouille
description: Classic Provençal vegetables slowly cooked into a rich tomato sauce.
steps: Dice all the vegetables evenly.
Heat olive oil in a large pan.
Cook the onion and garlic.
Add the remaining vegetables.
Pour in the passata.
Season with oregano.
Simmer for 30 minutes.
Finish with fresh basil before serving.
tip: Great as a side dish or served with grilled fish.

name: Halloumi & Roasted Vegetable Couscous
description: Roasted Mediterranean vegetables with grilled halloumi and fluffy couscous.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Chop the vegetables into even pieces.
Toss the vegetables with olive oil and seasoning.
Roast for 25 minutes.
Prepare the couscous according to the packet.
Grill the halloumi until golden on both sides.
Mix the vegetables with the couscous and basil.
Top with the halloumi and a squeeze of lemon.
tip: Excellent served warm or cold for lunch.

name: Mediterranean Tuna Pasta
description: Wholemeal pasta with tuna, tomatoes, olives and fresh herbs.
steps: Cook the pasta until al dente.
Heat the olive oil in a frying pan.
Cook the garlic for one minute.
Add the tomatoes and tomato purée.
Stir through the tuna and olives.
Drain the pasta and add to the sauce.
Fold through the basil.
Serve immediately.
tip: Add chilli flakes for a gentle kick.

name: Lemon Garlic Prawn Orzo
description: Light Mediterranean orzo with prawns, garlic and fresh lemon.
steps: Cook the orzo until tender.
Heat the olive oil in a large pan.
Cook the garlic until fragrant.
Add the prawns and cook until pink.
Stir in the tomatoes.
Drain the orzo and add to the pan.
Finish with parsley and lemon juice.
Serve immediately.
tip: Don't overcook the prawns—they only need a few minutes.

name: Chicken Shawarma Bowls
description: Middle Eastern inspired chicken served over couscous with fresh salad and yoghurt.
steps: Mix the spices with olive oil.
Coat the chicken thoroughly.
Cook the couscous according to the packet.
Grill the chicken until cooked through.
Prepare the salad vegetables.
Slice the cooked chicken.
Assemble the bowls with couscous, salad and chicken.
Finish with Greek yoghurt and coriander.
tip: Excellent for meal prep.

name: Healthy Chicken Gyros
description: Greek-style chicken served with salad and yoghurt.
steps: Season the chicken.
Cook until golden and fully cooked.
Prepare the salad.
Mix yoghurt with lemon and herbs.
Slice the chicken.
Warm the serving base if using.
Assemble with salad and chicken.
Drizzle over the yoghurt dressing.
tip: Also works well in wraps.

name: Turkey Meatballs in Tomato Sauce
description: Lean turkey meatballs simmered in a rich tomato sauce.
steps: Mix the turkey, egg and breadcrumbs.
Shape into meatballs.
Brown the meatballs.
Cook the garlic.
Add the passata.
Return the meatballs to the pan.
Simmer until cooked through.
Finish with parsley.
tip: Serve with wholewheat pasta.

name: Baked Feta Pasta
description: Creamy baked feta with roasted tomatoes stirred through pasta.
steps: Preheat the oven.
Place feta and tomatoes into a baking dish.
Add garlic and olive oil.
Bake until soft.
Cook the pasta.
Mash the feta into the tomatoes.
Stir through the cooked pasta.
Finish with basil.
tip: Add spinach just before serving.

name: Spinach & Chickpea Curry
description: A healthy, lightly spiced curry packed with chickpeas and spinach.
steps: Cook the onion and garlic.
Add the spices.
Pour in the tomatoes.
Add the chickpeas.
Simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir through the spinach.
Cook until wilted.
Serve immediately.
tip: Serve with brown rice for a larger meal.

name: BBQ Mediterranean Chicken Kebabs
description: Marinated chicken kebabs with peppers and red onion, perfect for the barbecue.
steps: Cube the chicken evenly.
Mix the marinade ingredients.
Coat the chicken and chill for 30 minutes.
Thread onto skewers with peppers and onion.
Preheat the barbecue.
Cook the kebabs, turning regularly.
Ensure the chicken reaches 75°C.
Serve with salad and lemon wedges.
tip: Leave a little space between each piece for even cooking.

name: Honey Mustard Salmon
description: Oven baked salmon glazed with honey and wholegrain mustard.
steps: Preheat the oven.
Mix the honey and mustard.
Season the salmon.
Brush over the glaze.
Bake until just cooked.
Rest for two minutes.
Sprinkle with parsley.
Serve immediately.
tip: Try serving with steamed broccoli.

name: Garlic Herb Pollock
description: Lightly pan-fried pollock with garlic, parsley and lemon.
steps: Pat the fish dry.
Season both sides.
Heat the olive oil.
Cook the pollock until golden.
Add the garlic.
Finish with parsley.
Squeeze over lemon.
Serve immediately.
tip: Avoid moving the fish too early so it develops a golden crust.

name: Mediterranean Stuffed Peppers
description: Roasted peppers filled with couscous, vegetables and feta.
steps: Halve the peppers.
Roast for 10 minutes.
Cook the couscous.
Cook the onion and tomatoes.
Mix with couscous and feta.
Fill the peppers.
Bake until golden.
Finish with basil.
tip: These make excellent leftovers.

name: Greek Turkey Meatball Traybake
description: Turkey meatballs baked with tomatoes, peppers and feta.
steps: Mix the turkey, egg and breadcrumbs.
Shape into meatballs.
Arrange in a baking dish.
Add the vegetables.
Drizzle with olive oil.
Bake until cooked through.
Scatter over feta.
Finish with parsley before serving.
tip: Serve with a simple green salad.

name: Mediterranean Chicken & Bean Skillet
description: Chicken breast cooked with cannellini beans, tomatoes and fresh herbs in one pan.
steps: Season the chicken.
Brown in olive oil.
Add the garlic.
Stir in the tomatoes and tomato purée.
Add the cannellini beans.
Simmer until the chicken is cooked.
Finish with basil.
Serve straight from the pan.
tip: Great with crusty wholemeal bread.

name: Smoky Cod & Butter Bean Stew
description: A hearty Mediterranean fish stew with butter beans and smoked paprika.
steps: Cook the onion and garlic.
Add the smoked paprika.
Pour in the tomatoes.
Add the butter beans.
Simmer for 15 minutes.
Nestle in the cod.
Cook until the fish flakes.
Finish with parsley.
tip: A squeeze of lemon lifts the flavours.

name: Mediterranean Rice Bowl
description: Brown rice topped with roasted vegetables, chickpeas and feta.
steps: Cook the brown rice.
Roast the vegetables.
Warm the chickpeas.
Season everything well.
Divide the rice between bowls.
Top with vegetables.
Scatter over feta.
Finish with basil and olive oil.
tip: Ideal for packed lunches.

name: Lemon Dill Fish Cakes
description: Light fish cakes flavoured with fresh dill and lemon.
steps: Blend the fish.
Mix with egg and breadcrumbs.
Add dill and lemon zest.
Shape into fish cakes.
Chill for 15 minutes.
Pan fry until golden.
Turn carefully.
Serve hot with salad.
tip: Serve with Greek yoghurt.

name: Will's Grill Mediterranean BBQ Platter
description: A mixed barbecue platter of chicken, halloumi and grilled vegetables.
steps: Prepare all the vegetables.
Season and cube the chicken.
Slice the halloumi.
Heat the barbecue.
Cook the chicken until fully cooked.
Grill the vegetables and halloumi.
Arrange everything onto a serving platter.
Finish with basil and fresh lemon.
tip: Perfect for sharing outdoors.
